title: rootless: support launching slirp4netns without --disable-host-loopback
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**


/kind feature

**Description**

Support launching slirp4netns without `--disable-host-loopback`, so that the localhost ports on the host can be accessed as 10.0.2.2 from containers.

For security, the default MUST remain to use --disable-host-loopback.

Originally requested by @tmds https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/1733#issuecomment-655969836

Corresponding code: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9eac75a967e3459b32b1acd220afb49b60f5e5aa/libpod/networking_linux.go#L218-L220

comments.body: Sure, what would you suggest the CLI for this should be?
I'm still of the opinion we need a generic network options CLI - `--network-opt slirp4netns:enable-host-loopback,disable-seccomp` or similar

It would be nice to be able to pass options to `slirp4netns` from the command line.

First class features should also be exposed through direct arguments, without referencing `slirp4netns`.

let's add new flags for slirp4netns using: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/6965

These flags are slirp4netns specific, they should not be exposed through a global argument.

I've added a patch to #6963 

@AkihiroSuda will this help with rootless kind?

I guess you meant #6965?

> rootless kind

I'm not sure. At least kind with rootless Docker seems functional without this. Probably rootless Podman doesn't require this either.
Yes I think the main issue is that with podman containers cannot communicate with each other when running rootless

@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe I'm not sure how to use this.

I have a webserver running on localhost:
```
$ curl http://localhost:5000
Hello World!
```

I create a container that is on a network. I'd like the container to be able to the localhost webserver.
```
$ podman network create mynet
$ podman run -ti --network mynet busybox /bin/sh
/ # wget http://10.0.2.2:5000
Connecting to 10.0.2.2:5000 (10.0.2.2:5000)
wget: can't connect to remote host (10.0.2.2): Network is unreachable
```

What argument should I add, and to which command?
`podman run --network slirp4netns:allow_host_loopback=true`. Requires Podman 2.1.0 or later.

I'd like the container to be connected to a network, and still be able to access localhost. I'm not able to ping the other containers on the network when I add the `allow_host_loopback=true`.

```
$  podman run -ti --network mynet busybox /bin/sh
/ # ping other_container
PING other_container (10.88.23.8): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.88.23.8: seq=0 ttl=64 time=0.098 ms
64 bytes from 10.88.23.8: seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.107 ms
^C
--- other_container ping statistics ---
2 packets transmitted, 2 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.098/0.102/0.107 ms
/ # exit
$  podman run -ti --network mynet --network slirp4netns:allow_host_loopback=true busybox /bin/sh
/ # ping other_container
ping: bad address 'other_container'

```
Thanks! I've created a feature request for it: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7888.
